checks.comment: No variability classification in Gaia DR3 vari tables. At P = 1352 d and moderate eccentricity (e = 0.532), tidal modulation near periastron may be present.  At G = 12.3 (bright), photometric precision is excellent and any large-amplitude variability would have been detected.

At G = 12.3, the source is relatively bright.  At low Galactic latitude (b = 2.8 deg), source density is elevated compared to the halo, but RUWE = 9.2 is fully consistent with binary photo-centre motion.  The bright apparent magnitude reduces contamination sensitivity.

checks.tanikawa_et_al: Tanikawa et al. (2023, ApJ, 946, 79) reported Gaia DR3 black hole candidates from astrometric+spectroscopic solutions.  A 1352-day AstroSpectroSB1 candidate with a red giant primary matching this system's properties appears in their catalogue.  They argued for a dark companion above the neutron-star limit and called for follow-up confirmation.  This source is therefore NOT a novel discovery; our analysis provides independent characterization with extinction correction, self-consistent stellar modeling, proper uncertainty propagation (including parallax inflation), and a comprehensive alternative-scenario assessment.
checks.comment: This source was previously identified as a BH candidate by Tanikawa et al. (2023).  Our analysis provides independent, more detailed characterization.  No other published work provides a dedicated single-target analysis.

checks.eRASS1: Source at l=310.4 deg is within eRASS1 western hemisphere footprint (l=180-360).  No catalogued detection at this position.
checks.VLASS: VLASS (1-4 GHz) covers this position (Dec > -40 deg NOT applicable; source at Dec = -59 deg is OUTSIDE VLASS footprint).
checks.RACS: RACS (Rapid ASKAP Continuum Survey, 888 MHz) covers the southern sky.  No catalogued detection at this position (typical rms ~0.25 mJy/beam).
checks.SUMSS: No match within 30 arcsec in SUMSS (843 MHz)
checks.comment: No X-ray detection in ROSAT, XMM, or eROSITA eRASS1.  Upper limit L_X < 5.3e+30 erg/s (0.2-2.3 keV, eRASS1) at d ~ 1488 pc.  No radio detection in RACS or SUMSS.  All non-detections are consistent with a quiescent (non-accreting) BH in a wide, detached system with P = 1352 d.  At periastron, a(1-e) ~ 2.7 AU is much larger than the Roche lobe of the K-giant primary.

At l = 310.4 deg, b = 2.8 deg, d = 1488 pc, z = 0.072 kpc above the plane.  With |z| ~ 72 pc, this source is firmly in the Galactic thin disk.  The measured radial velocity (RV = -5.04 +/- 2.08 km/s) is unremarkable for a disk star, consistent with normal Galactic rotation.  Thin disk membership suggests formation through standard stellar evolution of a massive binary progenitor.

summary: All archival veto checks passed.  Thin disk membership at |z| ~ 72 pc.  Measured RV available.  No prior compact-object identification.
